DAC_Error_Focus_Usability_01 (AAA)


Description

Summary
When users submit forms with validation errors, focus remains on the “Save and continue” button instead of moving to the error summary. This behaviour is inconsistent with the Welcome to GOV.UK  Design System and causes significant usability issues for screen reader and voice activation users.

Description
Across multiple pages within the Catch Certificate and Non-Manipulation Document journeys, if a user submits a form without selecting a required radio option (or leaving required fields blank), the page displays an error summary at the top but keyboard focus does not move to it.

Instead, focus remains on the “Save and continue” button. This makes it difficult for users of assistive technologies to understand that an error has occurred and to navigate to the relevant fields using the error summary links.

User impact
Screen reader feedback (JAWS):

“Submitting the page with errors does not direct my position to the updated content as expected with JAWS, as my position remains on the ‘continue’ option if selecting ‘continue’. I found this disorientating, as I would expect to be directed to the error messages which appear at the top of the form.”

Voice activation user feedback:

“When checking for errors on this page by leaving all the input fields blank and pressing the ‘Save and continue’ button the page moves up slightly to the errors, however my focus is not directed to them first. I would expect error links to receive focus when errors are present.”

Steps to reproduce
Navigate to one of the affected pages.

Leave required fields blank (for example, do not select a radio option).

Select “Save and continue”.

Observe that:

An error summary is displayed at the top of the page.

Keyboard focus remains on the “Save and continue” button instead of moving to the error summary.

Expected behaviour
When validation errors occur:

Focus moves automatically to the error summary.

Screen reader users are informed of the errors immediately.

Users can tab or use error summary links to jump directly to inputs in error.

Actual behaviour
Error summary is rendered visually.

Keyboard focus remains on the submit button.

Users must manually navigate back to the top of the page to discover the errors.

Affected pages
Catch Certificate – Journey 1

Solution

Ensure that when validation errors are present:

Focus is programmatically moved to the error summary container.

The error summary is focusable (for example, using tabindex="-1").

Behaviour aligns with the Welcome to GOV.UK  Design System guidance.

Acceptance criteria
On form submission with validation errors, focus automatically moves to the error summary.

Screen reader users are immediately informed that errors have occurred.
